summary:
The preferred choice between available solvents for dissolving Gutta Percha based on both efficacy and safety considerations seems to favor \boxed{xylene}, given no specific harmful side-effects are reported against this compound when compared across alternatives such as n-hexane, chloroform, etc., which have been linked to various health risks including neurotoxicity issues. This conclusion aligns well within current dental practice guidelines emphasizing patient welfare alongside effective treatment outcomes through material selection processes involving biocompatibility assessments during endodontic procedures utilizing root canal obturation techniques employing thermoplasticized guttapercha systems dissolved via appropriate chemical means ensuring minimal risk exposure throughout procedure execution phases from start till completion stages inclusive thereof.
